evals.prompt: Give me a full overview of my network stats
evals.expected_output: Dashboard-style summary covering all stats endpoints
expectations: Calls /api/stats/global for total threats blocked
Calls /api/stats/bandwidth for recent bandwidth usage
Calls /api/stats/alerts for block counts and category breakdown
Calls /api/stats/summary for peak bandwidth per tunnel
Presents results in a concise table or structured dashboard format

evals.prompt: Show my bandwidth usage for the last hour
evals.expected_output: Per-tunnel and aggregate bandwidth stats for the past hour
expectations: Calls /api/stats/bandwidth with appropriate start/end or default 1-hour range
Authenticates with token parameter
Presents peak_in and peak_out in Mbps
Shows per-tunnel breakdown and aggregate totals

evals.prompt: What's being blocked on my network?
evals.expected_output: DNS block summary with category breakdown
expectations: Calls /api/stats/alerts with last=1d or similar recent range
Shows block_count and category_counts (ads, tracking, malware)
Highlights the most blocked categories
Mentions total blocks across all tunnels

evals.prompt: Give me a monthly summary of my network
evals.expected_output: 30-day peak bandwidth and alert totals per tunnel
expectations: Calls /api/stats/summary with days=30
Shows peak_in_mbps and peak_out_mbps per tunnel
Shows alert_totals broken down by type
Calls list_tunnels to get tunnel names for labeling

evals.prompt: Are there any security threats? Show me the last week
evals.expected_output: Security alerts filtered to the past 7 days
expectations: Calls /api/stats/alerts with last=7d
Focuses on security_event, dangerous_port, port_scan, doh_bypass types
Groups and summarizes by alert type
Highlights any concerning patterns or high counts
